words.definition: A set of words believed to have magical power.
words.example: Spells are the hardest thing in the world to get right.
words.exampleText: Spells are the hardest thing in the world to get right.

words.definition: An object or phrase believed to bring good luck or have magical powers.
words.example: Anyone can hang up a charm, but when it comes to making that charm...
words.exampleText: Anyone can hang up a charm, but when it comes to making that charm...

words.definition: Not able to occur, exist, or be done.
words.example: Everything has to be just right, or the most impossible things happen.
words.exampleText: Everything has to be just right, or the most impossible things happen.

words.definition: To walk in a leisurely way.
words.example: Parties of young men and women happen to meet when they are strolling on the wide street.
words.exampleText: Parties of young men and women happen to meet when they are strolling on the wide street.

words.definition: A person or group competing with another for the same objective or superiority.
words.example: The great houses of Petrocchi and Montana are bitter rivals.
words.exampleText: The great houses of Petrocchi and Montana are bitter rivals.

words.definition: A disrespectful or scornfully abusive remark or action.
words.example: A Petrocchi insulted me, Rinaldo explained with his most flashing smile.
words.exampleText: A Petrocchi insulted me, Rinaldo explained with his most flashing smile.

words.definition: Causing shock or dismay; horrific.
words.example: If they fight with spells, it can be appalling.
words.exampleText: If they fight with spells, it can be appalling.

words.definition: A large meal for many people.
words.example: He promptly invited all the Petrocchis to a feast.
words.exampleText: He promptly invited all the Petrocchis to a feast.

words.definition: The act of throwing or sending out something, often related to spells or magic.
words.example: He rolled the letter and cast one of his strongest spells over it.
words.exampleText: He rolled the letter and cast one of his strongest spells over it.

words.definition: A child of one's aunt or uncle.
words.example: They told it themselves to six smaller cousins as they grew up.
words.exampleText: They told it themselves to six smaller cousins as they grew up.

words.definition: A flat paper container used to enclose a letter or document.
words.example: A long cherry-colored scrip stamped with a black leopard.
words.exampleText: A long cherry-colored scrip stamped with a black leopard.

words.definition: A small enclosed space or stall for selling goods or providing services.
words.example: The Old Bridge in Caprona is lined with little stone booths.
words.exampleText: The Old Bridge in Caprona is lined with little stone booths.

words.definition: Lacking knowledge or awareness in general or about something specific.
words.example: Ignorant people think the envelopes can work magic.
words.exampleText: Ignorant people think the envelopes can work magic.

words.definition: To repeat or copy incorrectly.
words.example: The Petrocchis unkindly claimed that Rinaldo had misquoted his spell.
words.exampleText: The Petrocchis unkindly claimed that Rinaldo had misquoted his spell.

words.definition: A spell or object believed to cause love or attraction.
words.example: Everyone knew that all Rinaldo's spells were love-charms.
words.exampleText: Everyone knew that all Rinaldo's spells were love-charms.

words.definition: According to tradition; customarily.
words.example: That was a task traditionally left to the older brothers, sisters and cousins.
words.exampleText: That was a task traditionally left to the older brothers, sisters and cousins.

words.definition: A large meal or celebration, often with many guests.
words.example: He promptly invited all the Petrocchis to a feast.
words.exampleText: He promptly invited all the Petrocchis to a feast.

words.definition: Offensive or disrespectful.
words.example: He wrote old Francesco Montana a very insulting letter.
words.exampleText: He wrote old Francesco Montana a very insulting letter.

words.definition: Having many knots, especially in wood.
words.example: The great front gate was old knotty wood, just like their own.
words.exampleText: The great front gate was old knotty wood, just like their own.

words.definition: Producing powerful feelings or strong, clear images in the mind.
words.example: Lucia had a vivid imagination.
words.exampleText: Lucia had a vivid imagination.

words.definition: To be contrary to or not accepted by someone or something.
words.example: Nothing disagrees with a person so much as having to eat his own words.
words.exampleText: Nothing disagrees with a person so much as having to eat his own words.

words.definition: To develop from a child into an adult.
words.example: They told it themselves to six smaller cousins as they grew up.
words.exampleText: They told it themselves to six smaller cousins as they grew up.

words.definition: To move slowly and carefully around someone or something.
words.example: They turn their eyes aside and edge past as if walking past a pig-sty.
words.exampleText: They turn their eyes aside and edge past as if walking past a pig-sty.

words.definition: To place something on a hook or hanger.
words.example: Anyone can hang up a charm.
words.exampleText: Anyone can hang up a charm.
